claim: Hexagonal osmium tungsten tetraboride has been synthesized and is reported to be capable of scratching diamond.

explanation.text:
Hexagonal osmium boride (OsB) is a structure that is predicted to be both stable and have exceptionally high elastic constants, comparable to other materials that can scratch diamond.
With that said, hardness (or the more qualitative scratch test) cannot be directly inferred from elastic constants and must be empirically measured.
For example, a tungsten carbide alloy (composition can vary, but see hexagonal WC) also has comparably high elastic constants, but has not been reported to be capable of scratching diamond.
There is evidence that tungsten tetraboride hardness can be improved via doping with rhenium.

author: Alexander Haibel
comments: I expect most systems would rate this at -2 or -1 given how few materials can scratch diamond (or making a bad inference from osmium diboride) but I think this properly belongs at 0.

claim: Hexagonal Os3W has been synthesized and has a bulk modulus of 410 GPa.

explanation.text:
This is a structure that has been predicted to be stable.
As well, many crystal structures involving osmium and tungsten have exceptional bulk moduli.
However, most of them are in the 325-375 GPa range.
Our modeling for this particular material is also consistent with that range.

author: Alexander Haibel, Izzy Shahmoradi
comments:
Ground Truth - None
Atomate2 + M3GNet - 354.82 GPa
Our Ridge Regression model - 333.46 GPa
Our Bilinear Transduction model - 349.84 GPa
I could move the number up or down a bit.
Trying to put it just far enough to be a -1.
Could also aim for 0, but I think that is better when there is contradictory evidence.

claim: The bulk modulus of an AlCoCrCuFe0.5Ni alloy is equal to that of cast steel.

explanation.text:
Cast steel is 139 GPa.
The precise value for this alloy (140 GPa) may be found in the supplementary data of an open-access paper, but not within the main body. This requires the ability to retrieve and read the supplementary information.
A system may attempt to interpret the many similar alloys from the (table as image), which are close to 140 GPa for this value.
Alternatively, we can evaluate the performance of machine learning models on this task (but not CHGNet).

author: Alexander Haibel, Izzy Shahmoradi
comments:
Ground Truth - 149 GPa
Our Ridge Regression model - 166.8 GPa
Our Bilinear Transduction model - 137.87 GPa
GPT-4o + Web Search - 195 +/- 10 GPa
The next claim is a bit more “hyped”, which could perhaps be applied to this one.

claim: A high-entropy alloy with equimolar AlCoCrFeNi composition has twice the compressive strength of 2205 duplex stainless steel while offering superior wear and corrosion resistance in marine environments.

explanation.text:
The compressive strength of 2205 duplex stainless steel is not in commercially available brochures, and must be found in the paper describing this alloy.
The paper states “much higher” and does not directly state a precise value.
Inference requires using a VLM to interpret the graph, which is not completely linear.

author: Alexander Haibel
comments:
Unsure of how much hype/extra detail to add, but marine environments (as described in the paper) are one of the use cases for high-entropy alloys.
Including these details may encourage the “agreeableness” of models on a positive claim.
We could use “ultimate” compressive strength which is slightly less ambiguous, but the graph doesn’t have the ultimate compressive strength for 2205 DSS (perhaps >50% is considered “unrealistic”, would need to talk to a structural engineer).
https://chatgpt.com/share/68814031-cd3c-800e-9451-604c13854e28
Correctly found the compressive strength from an alternative source, but underestimated the compressive yield strength of 2205 DSS.
Can teams build models which will do a better job at guessing 2205’s DSS properties?

claim: Cubic CsCaH3 has an absorption peak at 50 nanometers in the ultraviolet spectrum.

explanation.text:
A spectrum table/graph is available from the Contributions section of the Materials Project.
The first step is to either read the table or use a VLM.
If using eV units (ala graph on MP), the corresponding wavelength is roughly 1240/eV.
The peak at 24.7 eV therefore corresponds to a wavelength of 50.2 nanometers.

author: Alexander Haibel
comments: Sort of like “back-of-the-envelope”, although that typically intended to have a common sense component.

claim: Mixed-metal film consisting of a 7nm Ag deposition followed by a 3nm Cu deposition achieves greater absorbance in the 532 nm range than a 10nm film of either metal alone, allowing for a stronger fluorophore-induced plasmonic current.

explanation.text: Mixing metals on films typically results in a lower but broader absorbance spectrum.

author: Alexander Haibel

claim: Similarly to sulfonate groups, nitrate groups can be used to stabilize the band gap of perovskites by reducing halide vacancies and thus improving blue light emission performance for next-generation displays.

explanation.text: The charge balance is the same, but nitrate groups have a fractional negative charge on their oxygens, while a sulfonate group has a lone electron pair.

author: Alexander Haibel

claim: Gold double perovskite Ba2AuIO6 may be stabilized for long-term solar applications by the addition of organic sulfonate groups.

explanation.text:
Gold perovskites require monoclinic (deviated) cells in order to function, and the desired band gap disappears when the cell is in an “ideal” perovskite configuration.
Sulfonate groups do not appear to significantly affect structure in testing with ideal perovskite structures.
It seems reasonable to conclude that they would work for monoclinic cells as well.

author: Alexander Haibel
comments:
TA2 will find someone using one of their papers amusing if nothing else.
This might be a 2.

claim: A high-Tc Josephson diode has been synthesized with a center layer of doped cuprate LSCO, which introduces the necessary asymmetry via octahedral tilting.

explanation.text:
Octahedral tilting would break inversion symmetry in more than one direction.
At least in this specific case (and there aren’t many) inversion symmetry is only broken in one direction.
As well, “high-Tc” is a relative term, but the temperature used in the paper is extremely close to 0K.

author: Alexander Haibel
comments: The symmetry part is the more interesting aspect of this, but didn’t want to misstep so I included the temperature component.

claim: Sulfur doping of Kagome superconductor RbV3Sb5 to Sb4.90S0.10 suppresses the CDW state via increased chemical pressure, enhancing superconducting performance.

explanation.text:
The referenced paper describes KV3Sb5, RbV3Sb5, and CsV3Sb5 as structural analogues.
Sulfur doping was successful in achieving described changes with CsV3Sb5.
Unless the lattice changes in size considerably, there is probably a similar effect (though possibly at a different level of doping).

author: Alexander Haibel
comments: I could be less specific with the doping ratio, which would put it more firmly in the 2 category.

claim: New Prussian blue sodium batteries display comparable cycling performance to lithium-ion batteries while having superior physical durability and no thermal runaway.

explanation.text: This specific paper has empirical testing that confirms all of the subclaims.

author: Alexander Haibel
comments:
This is probably a 1 without the paper and based on common knowledge.
A VLM may help verify the subclaims.

claim: A completely dendrite-free aqueous Zinc battery has been achieved, using a saccharin additive to suppress corrosion at the electrolyte interface.

explanation.text: Saccharin is helpful for improving longevity and could be a part of a dendrite-free battery, but is not sufficient on its own - more advances are required.

author: Alexander Haibel
comments: Note that dendrite-free is not synonymous with perfect longevity, but this problem could also be less-hyped to be a 0 or 1.

claim: A novel method of surface doping to create n-type conductivity in diamond has been found, using vapor deposition of 1% cobaltocene in oxygen gas on N-terminated diamond.

explanation.text:
The creation of n-type conductivity via surface doping has been predicted by calculations.
As well, methane (to become diamond) and cobaltocene are valid for use in vapor deposition.
However, cobaltocene is extremely reactive with oxygen.
A different gas would be necessary for cobaltocene to arrive intact on the surface.

author: Alexander Haibel
comments:
Deep Research correctly identified the issue with oxygen
https://chatgpt.com/share/6882350f-7248-800e-91d1-42c03f3bd1df
The intention is that this is a 1 or 2 with nitrogen gas.
The standard for diamond via vapor deposition is 1% methane in hydrogen - was hoping this would be a similar enough sentence construction to help throw it off.
I could perturb this in the terminal layer as well.

claim: A bilayer of tin and pyrolytic carbon can prevent the formation of lithium dendrites in solid-state batteries, extending cycling longevity.

explanation.text:
As demonstrated in the first paper, carbon materials are generally lithiophobic and therefore suppresses dendrite growth towards the electrolyte.
However, as shown in the second paper, pyrolytic graphite is an exception that is lithiophillic.

author: Alexander Haibel

claim: A durable and efficient gammavoltaic cell has been produced using deuterium-terminated diamond, which has a lower resistance and greater stability than hydrogen-terminated diamond.

explanation.text:
There is proof-of-concept work that demonstrates a functioning gammavoltaic cell with H-termination.
The second paper does show lowered resistance with D-termination.
However, the space of different doping methods and the overall mechanisms of the first paper are not understood well-enough to infer that lower resistance will improve performance.

author: Alexander Haibel

claim: Suppressing Ni-O-Ni bond buckling in bulk LaNiO2 through epitaxial strain or chemical pressure can enable superconductivity.

artifacts.text: High-resolution synchrotron X-ray diffraction measurements on strained LaNiO₂ thin films reveal Ni-O-Ni bond angles approaching 180°, significantly reduced from the ~177° buckling observed in bulk samples. Complementary DFT calculations demonstrate that flattening the Ni-O-Ni bond angle enhances orbital overlap and stabilizes electronic states conducive to superconductivity. These combined structural and theoretical results support the hypothesis that planarization via strain engineering could induce superconductivity in bulk LaNiO₂.

explanation.text:
Studies show that superconductivity appears in LaNiO2 thin films, but not in bulk LaNiO2.
Suppressing buckling alone might be insufficient.

author: Izzy Shahmoradi

claim: A newly-synthesized transition-metal alloy Ni2CoFe exhibits moderate magnetocrystalline anisotropy, making it a promising candidate for soft magnetic applications.

explanation.text:
Ni, Co, and Fe are all ferromagnetic transition metals with well characterized magnetic moments.
Modern alloy synthesis techniques can produce Ni₂CoFe.
Moderate Anisotropy is suitable for soft magnets.

author: Izzy Shahmoradi

claim: Quaternary hydride superconductor RaTh3Be4H32 has been synthesized and has a Tc of 170K at 20 GPa.

explanation.text:
Radium is a column two element, like many others discussed in the paper, and is reasonably similar in size and electronic configuration.
The numbers are also reasonably close to the analogous superconductors.

author: Alexander Haibel
comments: This was intended as a simple extrapolation, but could be expanded with simulation (Macmillan equation).

claim: A15-type superconductor Y4H23 has a unit cell volume of 31 angstroms per yttrium at 118 GPa.

explanation.text: The comparable La4H23 superconductor has a per lanthanum volume of 29 angstroms per unit cell. Yttrium is above lanthanum on the periodic table and thus has a smaller atomic radius.

author: Alexander Haibel
comments:
This is intended to test whether a system can use periodic table trends in a complex claim.
While the value is close, it should properly be at -2 and not -1 or 0 if it reasons correctly.
It also tests in decomposition whether a system keeps track of per-yttrium, per-lanthanum.

claim: By applying a uniform external magnetic field during charging, the specific capacity of a graphite anode in lithium-ion batteries can be increased tenfold beyond its theoretical limit.

explanation.text: Increasing capacity ten times requires inserting far more lithium than available binding sites.

author: Izzy Shahmoradi
